title: Cleome pakistanica
intro: Cleome pakistanica, also known as Cleome gynandra, is a species of flowering plant in the Cleomaceae family, commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of South Asia and Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its numerous health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleome pakistanica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been identified to possess significant biological activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Cleome pakistanica include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine systems.
taxonomy: Cleome pakistanica is a species of flowering plant in the family Cleomaceae. It belongs to the genus Cleome, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as spider flowers or bee nuts. The family Cleomaceae is a small family of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The classification of Cleome pakistanica places it as an annual or perennial herb, with a growth habit that varies depending on the specific environment and growing conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification and taxonomy of this species.

parts: Cleome pakistanica contains medicinal parts such as its leaves, roots, and flowers, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world. preparations: Cleome pakistanica is known for its medicinal properties, and herbal preparations have been used to treat various health issues.
